mcpBetaText: Our MCP server is still in beta. If you have any feedback or run into any issues, please let us know so we can continue improving it!
mcpSetupTitle: How to configure MCP in Claude Desktop
mcpStep1Desc: Node.js is required to run the MCP Server. If you don't have it yet, download and install it from
mcpNoKeyYet: Create an MCP key above first — the command will update automatically with your key.
mcpTerminalPaste: then paste and run the command below.
mcpStep3Desc: Restart Claude Desktop, then check that SEOGets appears in the list of Connectors. Try asking:
mcpStep3Example: "What are the top 3 countries on mysite.com?"
mcpTip: 💡 Tip: Always mention your site's domain in the first prompt so Claude knows which site to query.
sharingDesc: Share all your GSC sites with your team
superSitesDesc: Store your Google Search Console data for up to 5 years, and unlock advanced features such as Index Reporting and Top Positions filtering.
noGscMatch: No GSC match — press Enter or click + Add to add manually
noSuperSitesYet: No Super Sites yet. Search for a property above to get started.
modalFeedbackSubtitle: Share your thoughts — I read everything
modalThanksSubtitle: Your support keeps this project alive
feedbackHint: This will open Telegram with your message pre-filled.
telegramPreFilled: Your message is pre-filled — just hit send.
usdtNote: ⚠️ Only send USDT on the TRC-20 (Tron) network to this address.
devStory: Hey! I'm an independent developer who built SEO Gets as a free tool for everyone.
devStoryDetail: No VC funding, no team, no ads — just me and a lot of coffee. If this tool saves you time or money, a small donation means a lot and helps keep development going.
optContentDecayDesc: Find content that needs to be updated.
optCannibalizationDesc: Find pages that are competing against each other.
optStrikingDesc: Find keywords you can easily rank for.
optCtrDesc: See if your titles are performing well.
cdmHowItWorks1: The heatmap graph shows how each webpage performs over time, using color shading to highlight trends.
cdmHowItWorks2: It lets you quickly see which pages consistently do well and which ones might need updating to improve.
cdmHowItWorks3: This visual overview makes it easy to spot patterns across many pages and months, helping you focus your SEO work where it's needed most.
cdmWhatToDo1: Select a time frame that you want to target (we recommend 16 months).
cdmWhatToDo2: Set threshold to where you can start to see a wide range of color shades.
cdmWhatToDo3: Look for pages that used to perform well that have since dropped off or high performing pages to replicate in other ways.
cdmWhatToDo4: Pages that are refreshed or add new content by at least 30% have a significant increase in traffic on average.
cdmTableTitle: Decaying Pages That May Need Refreshing
kcHowItWorks1: This report highlights the queries where multiple URLs are in competition with each other.
kcHowItWorks3: Pages representing less than 10% of the total query impressions are excluded
kcHowItWorks4: Queries where the top two pages are more than 20 positions apart are excluded
kcWhatToDo1: Start by looking at the top query for each competing page. If both pages share the same top query, it's likely cannibalization — consider consolidating into a single, stronger page.
kcWhatToDo2: If the top queries are different, check the SERPs for each query. If results overlap, one page can probably rank for both. If the SERPs are distinct, you likely need separate pages.
kcWhatToDo3: Every case is different — review each issue individually before making changes.
kcEmptyDesc: There are no queries with multiple pages competing.
sdkPosRangeDesc: Only show queries ranking between these positions.
sdkWhatToDo1: Use this report to find queries that are close to ranking on the first page of Google.
sdkWhatToDo2: Improve the content of ranking pages to increase the position for these queries.
sdkEmptyDesc: There are no queries matching your filters.
ctrHowItWorks2: have shown that the first position on Google search results has an average of 27.6% CTR, which drops to 15.8% for the second position, etc.
ctrHowItWorks3: We compare your numbers against these benchmarks to help you understand which URLs are underperforming.
ctrHowItWorks4: Only queries in the top 10 positions are included in this report.
ctrImprove1: Write better meta descriptions and titles
ctrImprove2: Title tags between 40 and 60 chars have the best CTR
ctrImprove3: Positive titles have better CTR than negative or neutral
ctrImprove4: Improve your position on Google search results
ctrEmptyDesc: There are no queries in the top 10 positions yet.
setHelpTitle: The settings tab that actually helps your analysis
setHelpDesc: Set up these essential workflows in 2 minutes to automatically find your biggest SEO opportunities, track what's actually working, and create reports that impress clients.
setBrandMonitorDesc: Stop confusing brand searches with actual SEO wins—see your true organic performance.
setContentGroupsDesc: Group content to see what part of your site is growing and what's decaying.
setClientPortalDesc: Turn any report into a live, shareable link your clients can access anytime.
setBrandedDesc1: Set up branded keywords for {domain} to monitor your brand's performance in search results.
setBrandedDesc2: We recommend adding branded keywords that are unique to your brand, and are not generic terms.
setBrandedDesc3: We use Broad Match / Contains statement for these keywords. If you add ikea, it will match queries like ikea chair or ikea table.
setTopicDesc1: Topic Cluster is a collection of related queries or keywords.
setTopicDesc2: Grouping keywords allows you to analyze their performance much more efficiently than analyzing individually.
setContentDesc1: Grouping related pages allows you to analyze the performance of multiple pages much more efficiently.
setContentDesc2: Common use cases include a group for all blog posts, pages related to a similar topic, multiple landing pages, or programmatic SEO pages.
setSharedLinkDesc1: Share the {domain} dashboard with your clients using a public shared link. Here's an
setSharedLinkDesc3: You can also embed the shared link into reporting platforms, such as
setSharedLinkNote: Shared Links are impossible to guess, and you can remove them at any time.
setSuperSiteDesc1: Configure which pages you wish to monitor with our index reporting for site.
setSuperSiteDesc2: We check your sitemaps every 24 hours to discover new pages.
setUnlockSelection: and unlock Super Site Page Selection
setSitemapNote: Enter your preferred sitemap urls to override the automatic GSC/robots.txt sitemaps.
setDataSourceDesc: The data for {domain} dashboard is currently sourced from GSC.
setGscDesc: Free access to last 16 Months of data sourced directly from Google.
setExtendedDesc1: We store your GSC data for 5 years.
setBigQueryDesc: Bring your own BigQuery and unlock even more data. Chat with us to learn more.
setGa4Desc: Linking to a GA4 property will unlock additional metrics.
setGa4Note: GA4 data will be filtered by the following organic sources:
setGa4Sources: google / organic, chatgpt.com / (not set), duckduckgo / organic, bing / organic, yahoo / organic, chatgpt.com / referral, perplexity.ai / referral, ntp.msn.com / referral, yandex / organic, search.brave.com / referral, baidu / organic, ecosia.org / referral, qwant.com / referral, seznam / organic
annCreateFirstNote: Create your first note to get started
annEmptyDesc: You don't have any notes yet.
